current_executable_status: executable (code_ref swm.world_model_v2.registry.families.diffusion:contagion_window_predict + test_ref tests/test_diffusion_families.py resolve; fitted comparator on Higgs)

primary_sources.title: Talk of the Network: A Complex Systems Look at the Underlying Process of Word-of-Mouth
primary_sources.authors: Goldenberg, J.; Libai, B.; Muller, E.
primary_sources.what_it_provides: Independent-cascade / stochastic-cellular-automaton word-of-mouth formalism: each active tie transmits with a per-contact probability independently — the structural basis for the linear-in-exposure hazard lambda=q*k.

primary_sources.title: The Anatomy of a Scientific Rumor
primary_sources.authors: De Domenico, M.; Lima, A.; Mougel, P.; Musolesi, M.
primary_sources.what_it_provides: Source of the local Higgs Twitter diffusion dataset (~500k users, 1-7 July 2012; retweet/reply/mention/follower networks) on which the simple-contagion hazard was fitted as a baseline (SNAP higgs-twitter).

formal_model: Continuous-time survival hazard lambda_i(t) = q * k_i(t), where k_i(t) = number of already-active in-network neighbors (exposures) of ego i at time t; per-exposure transmissions are independent and additive (SI / independent-cascade limit). Adoption read from terminal survival state integrated event-by-event through the diffusion window.
causal_assumptions: Exposures act independently and additively on the hazard (no reinforcement, no saturation).
Exposure count k is exogenous to latent susceptibility (no homophily/confounding driving co-adoption).
SUTVA per exposure; one exposure's effect does not depend on others.
Network edges and activation times are observed without measurement error.
required_variables: exposure count k_i(t) = number of active neighbors
temporal_scale: Event-level hazard integrated over a diffusion window (hours to days; Higgs window ~168h).

reported_coefficients.name: q (per-exposure transmission rate, linear hazard)
reported_coefficients.context: Local Higgs train-split MLE of the linear form; this is a per-cascade nuisance rate, not a transportable constant.

reported_coefficients.name: linear-vs-concave held-out penalty (delta held-out loss)
reported_coefficients.context: The linear-in-exposure hazard was SIGNIFICANTLY WORSE than a concave/log-linear exposure response on Higgs held-out; simple contagion is retained only as a comparator, not the preferred predictor.

replication_evidence: SI / independent-cascade is a canonical, universally reproduced baseline across epidemiology and diffusion research, but is routinely outperformed by nonlinear (saturating / reinforcement) hazards on real cascades — as replicated locally on Higgs.
critiques_or_nulls: Higgs proved linear-in-k hazard is significantly worse than a concave exposure response (delta +0.00234 [0.00117,0.00349]).
Cannot distinguish contagion from homophily/confounding.
Ignores saturation, degree heterogeneity, and social reinforcement.
applicability_limits: Baseline/comparator only; appropriate where reinforcement and saturation are genuinely absent (e.g., simple pathogen or trivial-information spread).
transport_risks: q is per-campaign/per-network and requires refit for any new cascade.
Low structural risk but low predictive value — it is a known mis-specified shape for real social cascades.
best_validation_route: Retain as the null comparator inside the Higgs held-out survival benchmark; it has already lost to the concave form, which is the intended role.
planned_implementation: No new work — keep as the fitted linear comparator; ensure it remains the reference against which nonlinear forms are scored.
planned_parameter_packs: Higgs local q pack (fitted q=0.0224; comparator/nuisance)
promotion_blocker: No held-out WIN: the linear form is a comparator that lost to the concave exposure-response on Higgs (delta +0.00234). Eligible only as software_implemented baseline, not as a standalone validated predictor.

current_executable_status: executable (code_ref diffusion:contagion_window_predict + test_ref tests/test_diffusion_families.py; Hill form fitted on Higgs)

primary_sources.title: The Spread of Behavior in an Online Social Network Experiment
primary_sources.what_it_provides: Randomized controlled experiment: behavior (joining a health forum) spread FARTHER and FASTER on clustered-lattice networks than on matched random networks, and individual adoption was much more likely when a participant received social reinforcement from MULTIPLE neighbors — the causal anchor for reinforcement-dependent (complex) contagion.

primary_sources.title: Complex Contagions and the Weakness of Long Ties
primary_sources.what_it_provides: Formal theory distinguishing simple vs complex contagion; when adoption needs multiple independent confirmations, wide/long bridging ties become LESS effective and local clustering (redundant ties) becomes advantageous — the theoretical basis for the superlinear/threshold hazard shape.

formal_model: Hill hazard lambda_i(t) = exp(theta0) * k^alpha / (c^alpha + k^alpha). alpha>1 encodes superlinear onset (reinforcement: each additional distinct source raises the marginal effect), saturating at high k. alpha<=1 collapses toward simple/diminishing-returns contagion. Fitted by grid + profile likelihood.
causal_assumptions: Reinforcement from multiple DISTINCT sources causes adoption beyond what independent per-exposure transmission would predict.
The superlinear region must be separable from mere higher exposure and from homophily — Centola identifies this by RANDOMIZING network structure (not available observationally).
Distinct-source signals are approximately independent.
Network clustering is observed.
temporal_scale: Event-level hazard over the diffusion window (hours to days).

reported_coefficients.name: Hill exponent alpha (Higgs fit)
reported_coefficients.context: LOCAL NULL: alpha=0.75 < 1 means Higgs did NOT exhibit the alpha>1 complex-contagion (superlinear reinforcement) signature — the fitted shape was concave/saturating, consistent with simple diminishing returns, not reinforcement.

reported_coefficients.name: Hill intercept theta0 (Higgs fit)
reported_coefficients.context: Local Higgs baseline log-hazard; per-cascade nuisance.

reported_coefficients.name: Clustered-lattice vs random spread (Centola 2010)
reported_coefficients.context: Behavior spread farther and faster in clustered than random networks; adoption probability rose with the number of reinforcing neighbors. Paper reports the qualitative causal direction; a single transportable coefficient is not extracted here (null rather than fabricated).

datasets_in_literature: Centola 2010 online health-community experiment (artificial clustered vs random networks)
replication_evidence: Centola 2010 is the landmark randomized result; the mechanism is broadly supported theoretically and in follow-ups (e.g., reach-vs-reinforcement trade-off, arXiv:2411.07907), but the effect and the required alpha are context-dependent and have been debated. Observational support from Romero 2011. Locally NOT reproduced on Higgs (alpha<1).
critiques_or_nulls: Higgs fitted alpha=0.75 (<1): no superlinear reinforcement signature in this cascade.
The clustered>random result depends on artificial network manipulation; observational alpha is not causally identified (reinforcement vs homophily vs exposure volume are confounded).
Requires distinct-source counts that many datasets lack.
applicability_limits: Behaviors requiring social validation / costly or risky adoption (health behaviors, high-commitment norms).
Needs a clustered network AND multi-source exposure to manifest; poorly suited to trivial information.
Not expected on low-cost information cascades (consistent with Higgs alpha<1).
transport_risks: alpha is highly context-dependent (Higgs 0.75 vs Centola superlinear); do not transport across behaviors.
Depends on network clustering, which itself varies across platforms.
best_validation_route: Refit alpha per-cascade on Higgs held-out (done; sublinear). For a true positive test, validate against a dataset with manipulated/measured multi-source reinforcement (Centola-style clustered-vs-random) — not currently local.
planned_implementation: Keep Hill hazard; add a distinct-source (not just total-exposure) feature so alpha estimates reinforcement rather than exposure volume; document Higgs alpha<1 as the honest local null.
planned_parameter_packs: Higgs local Hill pack (theta0=-1.299, alpha=0.75; local null, reinforcement not present)
promotion_blocker: No local held-out PASS record and no transfer record; moreover Higgs alpha=0.75 fails to reproduce the alpha>1 reinforcement signature, so the Centola causal evidence is not locally realized. Blocked above software_implemented.

primary_sources.title: Threshold Models of Collective Behavior
primary_sources.what_it_provides: Defines an individual threshold as the proportion of the group one must see adopt before joining; shows aggregate outcomes are a knife-edge function of the threshold DISTRIBUTION (the 100-person riot example) — foundation of fraction-of-neighbors adoption rules.

primary_sources.title: A Simple Model of Global Cascades on Random Networks
primary_sources.what_it_provides: Networked threshold model + a cascade condition: global cascades occur when a percolating 'vulnerable cluster' exists; cascades are rare and occur in a bounded connectivity window, with bimodal cascade sizes at high connectivity.

formal_model: Deterministic threshold rule: node i adopts iff the fraction of its neighbors already active >= phi_i, with phi_i drawn from a heterogeneous threshold distribution F(phi). Watts: a node is 'vulnerable' if phi_i <= 1/degree_i; global cascade iff the vulnerable subgraph percolates (G'(1) >= z boundary).
causal_assumptions: Each actor has a stable, exogenous threshold (fraction of neighbors) that deterministically triggers adoption.
Thresholds are heterogeneous across actors; the DISTRIBUTION, not the mean, governs outcomes.
Network adjacency is fully observed; updating is synchronous/round-based.
No strategic anticipation or discounting of future adopters.
temporal_scale: Discrete adoption rounds / static equilibrium (cascade completes over the update horizon; scale set by updating cadence).

reported_coefficients.name: Granovetter knife-edge threshold example
reported_coefficients.context: Illustrative, not an empirical coefficient: shifting one actor's threshold can flip a crowd from ~0 to 100 participants. Reported as a structural property; no transportable number to fabricate.

reported_coefficients.name: Watts cascade window
reported_coefficients.context: Global cascades confined to an intermediate mean-degree window bounded by the vulnerable-cluster percolation condition; exact bounds depend on the threshold distribution, so left null.

datasets_in_literature: Watts 2002 simulated random/scale-free networks (analytic + Monte Carlo)
Granovetter 1978 stylized applications (riots, innovation, rumor, strikes, voting, migration)
replication_evidence: Theoretically robust and heavily reproduced in simulation (Watts cascade condition is a standard result). Empirical identification of individual thresholds from field data remains weak and rarely attempted.
critiques_or_nulls: Extreme (knife-edge) sensitivity to the threshold distribution — Granovetter's own central point; small mis-specification flips outcomes.
Thresholds are almost never directly observed.
Deterministic rule is brittle; describes equilibrium more than dynamics.
applicability_limits: Collective-action settings with observable local adoption fractions and genuine threshold heterogeneity.
Round-based / static-equilibrium framing; not continuous hazards.
transport_risks: Threshold distribution is non-transportable across populations.
Tiny changes in F(phi) flip cascade / no-cascade — inherently high transport risk.
local_refit_feasible: limited (Higgs has neighbor-fraction exposure but no per-user threshold ground truth; baby names have adoption fractions but no network — thresholds not point-identified locally)
best_validation_route: Posterior-predictive check on Higgs cascade-size distribution under a fitted threshold distribution (calibrate F(phi) to reproduce observed cascade sizes), rather than per-node threshold recovery.
planned_implementation: Keep the fraction-of-neighbors rule; add a fitted heterogeneous threshold distribution and validate via cascade-size PPC (not point thresholds).
planned_parameter_packs: Structural Granovetter/Watts pack (threshold distribution as a prior; no data-fitted numbers yet)
promotion_blocker: No validation record (validation:[]); thresholds are not identifiable from available local datasets (no per-user threshold ground truth). Blocked above software_implemented until a cascade-size PPC passes.

current_registry_status: absent (NO explicit registry family; distinct from reinforcement_learning, which is Q-learning)
current_executable_status: not_implemented (no code_ref / test_ref; mechanism partially captured by complex_contagion_hazard's Hill exponent alpha)

primary_sources.title: Differences in the Mechanics of Information Diffusion Across Topics: Idioms, Political Hashtags, and Complex Contagion on Twitter
primary_sources.authors: Romero, D. M.; Meeder, B.; Kleinberg, J.
primary_sources.venue: Proc. 20th International World Wide Web Conference (WWW '11), pp. 695-704
primary_sources.what_it_provides: Defines STICKINESS (probability of adoption given one-or-more exposures) and PERSISTENCE (relative extent to which REPEATED exposures keep having large marginal effect). Empirical finding: politically controversial hashtags are the MOST PERSISTENT — repeated exposures keep raising adoption (a complex-contagion signature) — whereas idioms are sticky but plateau. First large-scale observational validation that content types differ in complex-contagion behavior.

primary_sources.title: The Spread of Behavior in an Online Social Network Experiment
primary_sources.what_it_provides: Causal evidence that reinforcement from MULTIPLE distinct neighbors (not just more exposure) raises individual adoption probability — the mechanism this family would encode.

formal_model: Adoption probability increasing in the number of DISTINCT adopting sources s (not just total exposures k): p_adopt = g(s), g convex/persistent when repeated distinct exposures keep large marginal effect. Operationally equivalent to the complex_contagion Hill hazard when s is used in place of k; 'persistence' = the marginal-effect decay rate of successive exposures (Romero's exposure-curve ratio).
causal_assumptions: Adoption responds to the count of DISTINCT adopting sources, separable from raw exposure volume and from homophily.
Distinct sources are approximately independent signals.
Repeated exposure effect (persistence) is a property of the content/behavior, not of confounded audience selection.
required_variables: count of distinct adopting sources per ego
exposure curve (adoption prob vs number of exposures)
temporal_scale: Per-exposure, accumulating over hours to days.

reported_coefficients.name: Persistence ranking by topic (Romero 2011)
reported_coefficients.context: Political/controversial hashtags show the highest persistence (repeated exposures keep large marginal effect); idioms high stickiness but low persistence. Category-level curve ratios reported in-paper; no single transportable scalar extracted (null rather than fabricated).

reported_coefficients.name: Multi-neighbor reinforcement effect (Centola 2010)
reported_coefficients.context: Adoption 'much more likely' with reinforcement from multiple neighbors; qualitative causal direction, coefficient left null.

replication_evidence: Romero 2011 is the primary large-scale observational support; Centola 2010 the causal anchor. The distinct-source persistence effect is real but overlaps heavily with what complex_contagion_hazard already models.
critiques_or_nulls: Hard to separate 'distinct sources' from raw exposure count and from homophily in observational data.
No registry family exists; the mechanism is currently redundant with complex_contagion_hazard's alpha.
Adding a separate family risks double-counting reinforcement.
applicability_limits: Only where distinct-source counts are measurable and separable from total exposure.
Content/behavior types with genuine repeated-exposure persistence (controversial/political, costly norms).
transport_risks: Persistence strength varies strongly by content type / behavior cost.
Observational persistence conflates selection with reinforcement.
local_refit_feasible: yes-but-redundant (Higgs has exposure counts; distinct-source count derivable — but this reduces to the complex_contagion Hill fit, which gave alpha<1)
best_validation_route: Do NOT create a separate family first; validate reinforcement through complex_contagion_hazard alpha (using distinct-source counts) on Higgs held-out, and only split it out if the distinct-source signal is separable from total exposure and improves held-out loss.
planned_implementation: No standalone family unless justified: fold reinforcement into complex_contagion_hazard by feeding distinct-source count s instead of/alongside k; document Romero persistence and Centola reinforcement as the evidence base for that feature.
planned_parameter_packs: (none standalone) — reinforcement encoded via complex_contagion Hill pack using distinct-source counts
promotion_blocker: No registry entry, no code_ref, no test_ref — cannot even reach 'implemented'. Mechanism is currently redundant with complex_contagion_hazard; a separate family is not yet justified.

primary_sources.title: A New Product Growth for Model Consumer Durables
primary_sources.what_it_provides: The Bass diffusion model: hazard of first purchase = p + q*F(t), with p = coefficient of innovation (external influence) and q = coefficient of imitation (internal word-of-mouth), over a fixed finite population m; tested on 11 consumer durables. Closed-form S-curve adoption with saturation at m.

primary_sources.title: A Meta-Analysis of Applications of Diffusion Models
primary_sources.authors: Sultan, F.; Farley, J. U.; Lehmann, D. R.
primary_sources.what_it_provides: Meta-analysis of ~213 diffusion-model applications: mean coefficient of innovation p ~ 0.03 (SD ~0.03) and mean coefficient of imitation q ~ 0.38 (SD ~0.35) — the canonical published-research prior ranges for Bass p and q.

formal_model: Bass: f(t)/(1-F(t)) = p + q*F(t), giving F(t) = (1 - e^{-(p+q)t}) / (1 + (q/p) e^{-(p+q)t}); adopters at t = m*f(t) with fixed finite susceptible population m. p = external/innovation hazard, q = internal/imitation (word-of-mouth) hazard. Local closed-form window-probability variant closed_form_window_p implements the finite-susceptible saturation.
causal_assumptions: Fixed finite susceptible population m; every member eventually adopts (no repeat / no churn).
Adoption hazard = constant external term p + imitation term q proportional to cumulative adopters F(t).
Homogeneous mixing (every non-adopter equally exposed to prior adopters).
p, q constant over the diffusion horizon.
temporal_scale: Product/behavior life cycle: months-to-years for durables (Bass); decades for cultural adoption (baby names).

reported_coefficients.name: p (coefficient of innovation), meta-analytic mean
reported_coefficients.context: Published-research prior for the external-influence coefficient; note SD approximately equals the mean (heavy-tailed, not a tight point).

reported_coefficients.name: q (coefficient of imitation), meta-analytic mean
reported_coefficients.context: Published-research prior for the internal word-of-mouth coefficient; SD ~0.35 is nearly as large as the mean 0.38 — wide, product/country-dependent.

reported_coefficients.name: meta-analysis sample size
reported_coefficients.uncertainty_or_null: approx (reported as 'more than 200 product innovations' across 15 studies)

datasets_in_literature: Bass 1969: 11 consumer durables (e.g., TVs, refrigerators, freezers, air conditioners)
baby names 1880+ (local; cultural adoption trajectories) — natural local Bass test
replication_evidence: Among the most heavily replicated models in management science — hundreds of applications in Sultan-Farley-Lehmann alone and thousands since; the p/q ranges are stable enough to serve as a prior but too wide for point transport.
critiques_or_nulls: p and q are not stably transportable: q SD (~0.35) is close to its mean (~0.38).
Market size m is often unidentified before the adoption peak (fits before the inflection are unreliable).
Deterministic aggregate model with no individual heterogeneity; retrospective fits are over-optimistic.
Assumes no repeat adoption / no churn.
applicability_limits: First-purchase / durable adoption in a bounded market.
Cultural popularity adoption with a well-defined susceptible pool (e.g., naming cohorts).
NOT recurrent/repeat behaviors; NOT open/unbounded populations without an informative m prior.
transport_risks: p, q vary by product category, country, and era (large meta-analytic SDs).
Use the Sultan-Farley-Lehmann pack as a PRIOR, not a point transport; m must be re-identified per domain.
local_refit_feasible: yes (baby-name cumulative adoption curves 1880+ are an ideal local Bass fit; Higgs cumulative cascade is a secondary target)
best_validation_route: Fit Bass to baby-name adoption trajectories (per-name m, p, q), then TRANSFER-check the fitted p/q against the Sultan-Farley-Lehmann meta-analytic ranges (0.03 / 0.38) as an out-of-sample plausibility gate.
planned_implementation: Add a published_research Bass pack encoding p ~ (0.03, SD 0.03) and q ~ (0.38, SD 0.35) from Sultan-Farley-Lehmann as informative priors; fit a local baby-names pack and compare to the pack ranges for transfer validation.
planned_parameter_packs: published_research Bass pack: p mean 0.03 (SD 0.03), q mean 0.38 (SD 0.35) [Sultan-Farley-Lehmann 1990]
local baby-names Bass pack (per-name m,p,q — to fit)
promotion_blocker: No held-out / PPC record yet (validation:[]). Needs a local Bass fit (baby names) with a PASS to reach locally_validated, then a transfer check vs the Sultan-Farley-Lehmann pack to move toward transfer_validated.

current_executable_status: executable-but-quarantined (code_ref diffusion:fit_hawkes + test_ref tests/test_diffusion_families.py run; held-out count forecast FAILED vs Poisson on Higgs)

primary_sources.title: Spectra of Some Self-Exciting and Mutually Exciting Point Processes
primary_sources.what_it_provides: Original self-exciting point process: conditional intensity lambda(t) = mu + integral of a kernel over past events, so each event raises the rate of future events — the structural basis of self-excitation.

primary_sources.title: SEISMIC: A Self-Exciting Point Process Model for Predicting Tweet Popularity
primary_sources.authors: Zhao, Q.; Erdogdu, M. A.; He, H. Y.; Rajaraman, A.; Leskovec, J.
primary_sources.what_it_provides: Where Hawkes WORKS: predicting the FINAL retweet/reshare count of a single cascade from its early timestamps + node degrees; intensity = infectivity x accumulated self-excitation. Cascade-SIZE prediction, not aggregate multi-cascade count forecasting.

primary_sources.title: Feature Driven and Point Process Approaches for Popularity Prediction
primary_sources.authors: Mishra, S.; Rizoiu, M.-A.; Xie, L.
primary_sources.what_it_provides: Marked Hawkes for cascade popularity: estimates content virality, memory decay, and user influence; hybrid with features. Confirms the regime where Hawkes adds value = per-cascade total-popularity prediction.

formal_model: Self-exciting intensity lambda(t) = mu + alpha*omega * sum_{t_i<t} exp(-omega (t - t_i)) (exponential kernel; branching ratio = alpha). Fitted by EM/MLE. Marked variants (SEISMIC/Mishra) scale excitation by user degree/influence for final-size prediction.
causal_assumptions: Past events causally raise future intensity via a fixed kernel (endogenous clustering).
Branching ratio alpha < 1 required for stationarity/finite cascades.
No unmodeled exogenous shock drives the clustering that the self-excitation term absorbs.
Process approximately stationary within the fitting window.
temporal_scale: Sub-daily event stream (seconds-to-hours); forecast horizon 24-72h in the local test.

reported_coefficients.name: held-out 24-72h count-forecast MAE, Hawkes
reported_coefficients.context: LOCAL FAILURE: Hawkes MAE 1098.9 WORSE than the Poisson baseline MAE 973.0 on the Higgs aggregate count forecast.

reported_coefficients.name: held-out 24-72h count-forecast MAE, Poisson baseline
reported_coefficients.context: The simpler Poisson beat Hawkes — the failure that quarantines this family.

reported_coefficients.name: fitted branching ratio alpha (Higgs)
reported_coefficients.context: Near-critical (alpha->1): the fit sits at the edge of the explosive regime, explaining unstable extrapolation and the under-forecast (Hawkes total 7149 vs Poisson total 10144).

datasets_in_literature: Higgs Twitter aggregate activity stream (local; FAILED count forecast)
replication_evidence: Strong and widely replicated FOR per-cascade popularity/final-size prediction (SEISMIC, Mishra, HIP, many follow-ups). NOT universal: the local Higgs AGGREGATE 24-72h count forecast failed against Poisson, showing self-excitation does not help this forecasting task.
critiques_or_nulls: LOCAL FAILURE preserved: Higgs 24-72h aggregate count forecast MAE 1098.9 > Poisson 973.0.
Fitted branching alpha=0.95 is near-critical -> unstable extrapolation and under-forecast (7149 vs 10144).
Self-excitation conflates endogenous contagion with exogenous shocks/circadian structure.
Works for size-given-early-cascade, not aggregate forward count forecasting.
applicability_limits: Per-cascade FINAL-size prediction from early timestamps (SEISMIC/Mishra regime) — the ONLY regime with strong support.
NOT aggregate multi-cascade forward count forecasting (the task it failed on Higgs).
transport_risks: Kernel parameters are per-cascade; near-critical alpha makes transport explosive.
The aggregate-count forecasting transport has ALREADY FAILED locally — do not reuse for that task.
local_refit_feasible: yes (already fitted on Higgs — and it FAILED held-out vs Poisson; failure is the record)
best_validation_route: Failure preserved on the aggregate 24-72h count forecast (Higgs). The only route toward a lesser status would be a SEPARATE, re-scoped task: per-tweet final-retweet-size prediction on a cascade dataset (SEISMIC benchmark). Until such a scoped test passes, it stays quarantined.
planned_implementation: Do NOT reuse for aggregate count forecasting (preserve failure). If revisited, re-scope to per-cascade final-size prediction (SEISMIC/marked-Hawkes) as a distinct family/task with its own held-out gate; keep the quarantined aggregate-forecast pack as a recorded negative result.
planned_parameter_packs: Higgs Hawkes pack (mu=0.00322/s, branching_alpha=0.95, omega=0.001667/s) — QUARANTINED negative result, kept as recorded failure
promotion_blocker: QUARANTINED and MUST remain so: held-out 24-72h aggregate count forecast FAILED vs Poisson on Higgs (MAE 1098.9 > 973.0). This preserved failure blocks any promotion. It could be reconsidered as domain_restricted ONLY under a different, re-scoped task (per-cascade final-retweet-size prediction, SEISMIC regime) with its own passing held-out record — not the aggregate-count task it failed.

current_executable_status: executable (code_ref diffusion:fit_aging_tau + test_ref tests/test_diffusion_families.py; tau fitted on Higgs)

primary_sources.title: Novelty and Collective Attention
primary_sources.what_it_provides: Empirical measurement (1M Digg users): collective attention to a novel item decays with a STRETCHED-EXPONENTIAL law governed by a single novelty factor, with a characteristic decay time of approximately ONE HOUR on Digg news — establishes that novelty/attention has an intrinsic fading time scale.

primary_sources.title: TiDeH: Time-Dependent Hawkes Process for Predicting Retweet Dynamics
primary_sources.venue: Proc. 10th International AAAI Conference on Web and Social Media (ICWSM 2016)
primary_sources.what_it_provides: Operationalizes information aging inside a point process: a time-dependent Hawkes intensity with an explicit aging (memory-decay) term plus circadian modulation, fitted to retweet dynamics (parameters p0, r0, phi0, tau_m) — a methodological template for embedding a decay time constant in a diffusion hazard.

formal_model: Age-weighted effective exposure k_eff(t) = sum_j exp(-age_j / tau), where age_j = time since exposure j and tau is a fitted decay (e-folding) time; the hazard uses k_eff instead of raw exposure count. tau fitted on a grid by likelihood.
causal_assumptions: Susceptibility/attention to a given exposure decays with its age via a fixed time constant tau (intrinsic novelty decay).
Decay is separable from susceptible-population depletion (novelty, not just fewer targets).
tau is stationary within the window.
temporal_scale: Decay time constant spanning minutes-to-hours (Digg news, ~1h) to days (Higgs, tau=48h) to potentially weeks (cultural names).

reported_coefficients.name: fitted decay time constant tau (Higgs)
reported_coefficients.uncertainty_or_null: grid {6,12,24,48}h; best log-likelihood at 48h (grid boundary) — upper end not bracketed
reported_coefficients.context: Local Higgs e-folding decay time; sits at the top of the search grid, so it is a lower bound on the true tau (identification weak upward).

reported_coefficients.name: aging improvement over no-aging (paired delta Brier, Higgs held-out)
reported_coefficients.context: The aging term barely improves held-out Brier and the CI includes zero — MARGINAL local signal, not a clear win.

reported_coefficients.name: novelty decay characteristic time (Digg)
reported_coefficients.context: Platform-specific (Digg news); illustrates that the decay time scale is domain-dependent and NOT the same as the local Higgs 48h.

replication_evidence: The qualitative stretched-exponential/decaying-attention finding is reproduced across platforms (Digg, Twitter, YouTube), but the fitted time constant varies by orders of magnitude across domains, and the local Higgs aging gain is only marginal.
critiques_or_nulls: Local aging gain is marginal on Higgs (paired delta Brier +0.000281, CI touches 0).
tau=48h sits at the grid boundary — likely underestimated / upper end unidentified.
Decay is confounded with susceptible-pool depletion (can look like aging even without novelty decay).
applicability_limits: Attention/novelty-driven contexts (news, memes, rumors) where recency matters.
Time constant must be RE-FIT per platform; do not assume a universal half-life.
transport_risks: Half-life spans minutes (Digg news ~1h) to days (Higgs 48h) to weeks (cultural adoption) — tau is strongly domain-specific.
Transporting a tau across domains would badly mis-time the decay.
local_refit_feasible: yes (already fitted tau=48h on Higgs; re-fittable per dataset)
best_validation_route: Higgs held-out already run (aging gain marginal); strengthen by fitting the decay on a second local series (e.g., decline phase of dropped baby names) and checking the tau/half-life is consistent with an aging (not depletion) mechanism.
planned_implementation: Keep age-weighted exposure; widen the tau grid beyond 48h (currently boundary-limited); report tau as a per-domain fitted quantity with a wide prior; document Digg ~1h vs Higgs 48h as the transport warning.
planned_parameter_packs: Higgs local aging pack (tau=48h; grid-boundary, treat as lower bound)
promotion_blocker: Aging improvement over the no-aging model is marginal on Higgs (paired delta Brier +0.000281, CI95 includes 0); no PASS held-out record, so it stays 'implemented'. tau is also unidentified at the grid boundary.
